observations.polarity: House as divided structure vs. Heart as divided self
observations.summary: The four-room house in Hebron is metaphorically described as resembling a man's heart divided against itself, highlighting a tension between physical structure and emotional division.

left_evidence.quote: The four-room house in Hebron had become the shape of one man's heart, divided against itself.

right_evidence.quote: You might ask why a house would come to resemble something so treacherous as a human heart

bridge_evidence.quote: and if you are asking that question, then you are paying the right kind of attention to what you are reading.
